en.extract:
The Ethiopian thrush (Turdus simensis) is a species of bird in the family Turdidae. It is found primarily in Ethiopia, and also locally in Eritrea. It is a medium-sized thrush 20–22 cm long, with a brown back, two vertical dark brown bars on the face, a spotted breast and belly, and (for a thrush) an unusually short tail. Compared to the closely related groundscraper thrush (T. litsitsirupa) from further south in Africa, it is browner, less grey-coloured above, and has a less marked (though still obvious) face pattern.


Taxonomy
The Ethiopian thrush was formally described in 1837 by the German naturalist Eduard Rüppell from a specimen collected in northern Ethiopia. He used the scientific name Merula (Turdus) simensis. The specific epithet is from the type locality, the Simien Mountains.
This thrush was formerly often considered a subspecies of the groundscraper thrush. These two species, each other's closest relatives, are treated in a separate genus Psophocichla by some authors.

It is found primarily in [[Ethiopia]], and also locally in [[Eritrea]]. It is a medium-sized [[Turdus|thrush]] 20–22 cm long, with a brown back, two vertical dark brown bars on the face, a spotted breast and belly, and (for a thrush) an unusually short tail. Compared to the closely related [[groundscraper thrush]] (''T. litsitsirupa'') from further south in Africa, it is browner, less grey-coloured above, and has a less marked (though still obvious) face pattern.{{cite book | last=Hoyo | first=Josep del | title=All the Birds of the World | publisher=Lynx edicions | publication-place=Barcelona | date=2020 | isbn=978-84-16728-37-4 | page=686}}{{cite book | last1=Clement | first1=Peter | last2=Hathway | first2=Ren | title=Thrushes | publisher=A&C Black | publication-place=London | date=2000-11-30 | isbn=0-7136-3940-7 | page=88, 325–326}} ==Taxonomy== The Ethiopian thrush was [[species description|formally described]] in 1837 by the German naturalist [[Eduard Rüppell]] from a specimen collected in northern Ethiopia. He used the scientific name ''Merula'' (''Turdus'') ''simensis''.{{ cite book | last=Rüppell | first=Eduard | author-link=Eduard Rüppell | year=1835 | title=Neue Wirbelthiere zu der Fauna von Abyssinien gehörig | volume=Vögel| language=German | location=Frankfurt am Main | publisher=S. Jr | year=1964 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=10 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=185 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14486374 }} The specific epithet is from the [[type locality (biology)|type locality]], the [[Simien Mountains]].{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=''simensis'' | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=simensis | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=11 February 2023 }} This thrush was formerly often considered a [[subspecies]] of the groundscraper thrush.{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | date=January 2023 | title=Thrushes | work=IOC World Bird List Version 13.1 | url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/thrushes/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=11 February 2023 }}{{cite journal | last1=Kirwan | first1=R.B. | last2=Collar | first2=N. | last3=del Hoyo | first3=J. | last4=Boesman | first4=P.F.D. | year=2022 | title=Ethiopian Thrush (''Turdus simensis''), version 1.0 | editor-last=Keeney | editor-first=B.K. | journal=Birds of the World | location=Ithaca, NY, USA | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | doi=10.2173/bow.grothr2.01 }} These two species, each other's closest relatives, are treated in a separate genus ''Psophocichla'' by some authors. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Commons category}} {{Taxonbar|from1=Q117258356|from2=Q27631025|from3=Q31874716}} [[Category:Turdus]] [[Category:Thrushes]] [[Category:Ethiopian Highlands endemic bird fauna]] [[Category:Birds described in 1837]] {{Turdidae-stub}}
